title: What is Mutual Fund?
meta_description: Mutual funds pool money from investors to buy stocks, bonds, and other assets. Learn types, how they work, risks, and how to invest in India.

hero.one_liner: A mutual fund collects money from many investors and invests it in stocks, bonds, or both — managed by a professional fund manager

sections.heading: What is a Mutual Fund in Simple Words?
content: A mutual fund is like a common pot where many people put their money together. A professional fund manager then takes this pooled money and invests it in stocks (company shares), bonds (government/corporate debt), gold, or a mix — depending on the fund's objective.

You don't need to pick individual stocks yourself. The fund manager does the research, buying, and selling for you. In return, the fund charges a small annual fee called the expense ratio (typically 0.5% to 2% of your invested amount). Your investment is divided into 'units' — when the fund's investments grow in value, your units become more valuable.

Mutual funds in India are regulated by SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India), which means there are strict rules on transparency, reporting, and investor protection. As of 2026, over 4.5 crore Indians invest in mutual funds, and total AUM (Assets Under Management) exceeds ₹65 lakh crore.

rows.label: Equity Funds
rows.value: Invest in stocks. Higher risk, higher return potential. Best for 5+ year goals.

rows.label: Debt Funds
rows.value: Invest in bonds/fixed income. Lower risk, stable returns. Good for 1-3 year goals.

rows.label: Hybrid Funds
rows.value: Mix of equity + debt. Moderate risk. Good for beginners wanting balance.

rows.label: Index Funds
rows.value: Track a market index (Nifty 50, Sensex). Low fees, no fund manager bias. Great for beginners.

rows.label: ELSS Funds
rows.value: Equity funds with tax benefit under 80C. 3-year lock-in. Best tax-saving mutual fund.

rows.label: Liquid Funds
rows.value: Ultra-short-term debt funds. Almost no risk. Alternative to savings account for spare cash.

sections.heading: How to Invest in Mutual Funds

steps.title: Complete KYC (one-time)
steps.description: Sign up on any mutual fund app (Groww, Zerodha Coin, Kuvera, Paytm Money). Complete eKYC using PAN + Aadhaar. Takes 5-10 minutes.

steps.title: Choose a fund
steps.description: For beginners: start with a Nifty 50 Index Fund (lowest fees, diversified). Later add a Flexi Cap or ELSS fund as you learn more.

steps.title: Invest via SIP or lump sum
steps.description: SIP (monthly auto-invest) is recommended for most people. Lump sum works if you have a large amount and the market is not at an all-time high.

steps.title: Track and review annually
steps.description: Don't check daily. Review once a year — is the fund beating its benchmark? If consistently underperforming for 2+ years, consider switching.

sections.heading: Mutual Fund vs Fixed Deposit vs Stocks
sections.footnote: Returns are historical averages and not guaranteed.

faqs.question: Is mutual fund safe?
faqs.answer: Mutual funds are regulated by SEBI and your money is held by a custodian, so it can't be misused. However, the value of your investment can go up or down based on market performance. Debt funds are safer than equity funds. No mutual fund guarantees returns.

faqs.question: What is NAV?
faqs.answer: NAV stands for Net Asset Value. It's the price of one unit of the mutual fund. If a fund's NAV is ₹100 and you invest ₹1,000, you get 10 units. NAV changes daily based on how the fund's underlying investments perform.

faqs.question: What is expense ratio?
faqs.answer: It's the annual fee charged by the fund house for managing your money. A fund with 1% expense ratio charges ₹100 per year for every ₹10,000 invested. Lower is better — index funds typically charge 0.1-0.5%, active funds charge 1-2%.

faqs.question: Should I choose direct or regular plan?
faqs.answer: Always choose Direct plan. Regular plans include distributor commission (0.5-1% extra expense ratio), which eats into your returns. On apps like Groww or Kuvera, you automatically get Direct plans.

faqs.question: How much tax do I pay on mutual fund gains?
faqs.answer: Equity funds: 12.5% on gains above ₹1.25 lakh after 1 year (LTCG), 20% if sold within 1 year (STCG). Debt funds: Taxed at your income tax slab rate regardless of holding period.

disclaimer: This article is for educational purposes only. Mutual fund investments are subject to market risks. Read all scheme-related documents carefully. Consult a SEBI-registered financial advisor before investing.
